summary: This source is a scientific abstract outlining the design and goals of the Genome of the Netherlands (GoNL) project. Co-authored by Dorret I. Boomsma, it describes the utility of whole-genome sequencing on 250 parent-offspring trio-families to characterize DNA variation, reconstruct haplotypes, and create a reference panel for future genome-wide association studies.

principles.statement: Family-based designs provide unique advantages for characterizing genomic variation.
principles.rationale: Sequencing parent-offspring trios allows researchers to accurately reconstruct haplotypes, characterize complex structural variants, and establish the rate of de novo mutations.
principles.source_quote: The family-based design represents a unique resource to assess the frequency of regional variants, accurately reconstruct haplotypes by family-based phasing, characterize short indels and complex structural variants, and establish the rate of de novo mutational events.
